questions.question: When you set your browser window to 1280px wide and zoom to 200%, what is the effective viewport width? What about at 400% zoom?

feedback.a: Incorrect. This multiplies instead of divides. Zooming in means less content fits.
feedback.b: Incorrect. The physical viewport stays 1280px but the effective viewport decreases with zoom.
feedback.c: Correct. The effective viewport width is calculated by dividing the actual viewport by the zoom percentage. At 200%: 1280/2 = 640px. At 400%: 1280/4 = 320px.
feedback.d: Incorrect. These numbers don't follow the correct calculation.

questions.question: A web app has: (1) product comparison table, (2) testimonial cards side by side, (3) sidebar navigation, (4) blog article. Which is a valid exception to SC 1.4.10?

feedback.a: Correct. Data tables are an explicit exception because they require two-dimensional spatial relationships to convey meaning.
feedback.b: Incorrect. Testimonial cards are independent content blocks that can stack vertically.
feedback.c: Incorrect. Body text and paragraphs must reflow. Text content should never require horizontal scrolling.
feedback.d: Incorrect. Navigation menus must reflow. Sidebars should collapse to hamburger menus.

questions.question: A "Subscribe to Newsletter" button has height: 40px and overflow: hidden. When text spacing is adjusted, the text gets clipped. What would fix this?

options.text: Add a tooltip that displays the full text on hover

feedback.a: Incorrect. Reducing font size works against users who need text spacing adjustments.
feedback.b: Incorrect. Tooltips don't solve the core problem of clipped visible text.
feedback.c: Incorrect. SC 1.4.12 requires no loss of content. Users need to understand what they are subscribing to.
feedback.d: Correct. Using min-height allows the button to grow when line height increases while maintaining its minimum size.

questions.question: According to SC 1.4.12, which text spacing values must designs support?

options.text: Only line height adjustments up to 1.5x
Line height 1.5x font size, letter spacing 0.12x, word spacing 0.16x, paragraph spacing 2x
Line height 2.5x, letter spacing 0.5x, word spacing 0.5x, paragraph spacing 4x
Only letter spacing and word spacing adjustments

feedback.a: Incorrect. SC 1.4.12 requires all four properties: line height, letter spacing, word spacing, AND paragraph spacing.
feedback.b: Correct. SC 1.4.12 specifies these exact minimum values.
feedback.c: Incorrect. These values exceed the requirements. Only the values in option B are required.
feedback.d: Incorrect. All four spacing properties must be supported.

questions.question: A recipe app locks to portrait orientation because "it looks better." Does this meet SC 1.3.4?

options.text: No, "looks better" is preference, not essential function
Yes, because the designer can determine the optimal experience
No, all apps must default to landscape orientation first
Yes, because cooking apps are typically used in portrait mode

feedback.a: Correct. SC 1.3.4 requires content to work in both orientations unless a specific orientation is essential for function.
feedback.b: Incorrect. Designer preference does not override accessibility requirements.
feedback.c: Incorrect. The requirement is not to prefer one orientation. Apps must work in BOTH orientations.
feedback.d: Incorrect. Typical usage does not create essential requirements. Users with mounted devices may have fixed orientations.
